text: We present a case of acute appendicitis with appendiceal rupture and abscess formation. Our patient is a fifty-four year old with no chronic illness. He was well until he presented to the emergency department with a history of abdominal pain of five day duration that was gradual in onset beginning peri-umbilical then migrating to the right lower quadrant. The pain did not radiate but was associated with nausea and vomiting. He did not reveal any aggravating or relieving factors. He did complain of intermittent fever and night sweats. Initial examination revealed a dehydrated patient with blood pressure of one-thirty over eighty-four and pulse rate of one hundred and ten per minute. Abdomen was tender in the right lower quadrant and suprapubically with guarding and rebound tenderness. His laboratory results were all normal except for an elevated WBC of twenty five point one. He had a CT scan of the abdomen which confirmed a ruptured appendicitis with abscess formation.

The patient is positioned supine with arms at the sides for a single port laparoscopic appendectomy. A vertical transumbilical skin incision is made, revealing the peritoneum. Vicryl 2-0 stay sutures are placed at the fascial end to aid port introduction. A gel point port is inserted through the incision, and pneumoperitoneum is established at 15 mm Hg. A 10-12 mm trocar and two 5 mm trocars are inserted in a triangular configuration. The 10 mm, 30-degree laparoscope is positioned at the seven o'clock port. A straight grasper is used for lateral retraction at the five o'clock port, while the twelve o'clock port serves as the working port. The abscess cavity is dissected out.

The surgeon frees the appendix from the abscess cavity and performs dissection of the mesoappendix, dividing the appendicular artery with a Maryland ligature. The jaws are carefully positioned away from the bowel wall to prevent injury. The base of the appendix is divided using a 35 mm linear stapler. The abdominal cavity is thoroughly irrigated, and the appendix is removed without a specimen bag.

The surgeon removes the appendix through the gel point system after disengaging the cap. The ALEXIS wound protector isolates the wound edge, and the incision is closed in layers using 2-0 Vicryl for the fascia and 3-0 Monocryl for the skin.
